project_overview.project_name: Response - Recovery - Resilience for Conflict-Affected Communities in Ethiopia Project (3R-4-CACE Project)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To (i) rebuild and improve access to basic services and climate-resilient community infrastructure and (ii) improve access to multi-sectoral response services for Gender-Based Violence (GBV) survivors; in selected conflict-affected communities in Ethiopia.
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 5.2 million direct beneficiaries in conflict-affected communities, including IDPs, host communities, and GBV survivors

geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with initial focus on 5 regions
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - all target regions are highly impacted by recent conflict and host large numbers of IDPs

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Rebuilding and Improving Access to Basic Services and Climate-resilient Community Infrastructure
key_components.description: Provision of rapid response basic services (health, education, WASH), community-based recovery activities through participatory planning, and strengthening institutions for resilience

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Improving Access to GBV Response Services
key_components.description: Expanding and strengthening GBV services in conflict-affected communities, GBV prevention and behavior change programming, and support to coordination and policy development
Project management costs and learning activities to improve effectiveness and adapt to changing settings
Zero-dollar component allowing rapid reallocation of funds in case of eligible emergency

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Finance (MoF) as lead implementing agency; Ministry of Women and Social Affairs (MoWSA) for Component 2
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Federal Steering Committee (FSC), Federal Project Coordination Unit (FPCU), Federal Project Implementation Unit (FPIU), Regional Steering Committees, Woreda Project Coordination Teams, Neighborhood Relations Committees
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Differentiated approach: government systems in Non-High Risk of Ongoing Conflict (NROC) Woredas; third-party implementation entities in High Risk of Ongoing Conflict (HROC) Woredas
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: FPCU responsible for overall M&E, supplemented by third-party monitoring in HROC areas, use of GEMS technology for remote monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Violent conflicts, military escalation, government unwillingness to channel resources to conflict areas, access restrictions
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Third-party implementation in HROC areas, direct World Bank payments to third parties, Security Risk Assessment and Management Plan, periodic conflict assessments

identified_risks.risk_description: Economic imbalances, inflation surge, disrupted supply chains, limited access to external financing
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Focus on small-scale investments, reliance on local resources

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak procurement and FM capacities at national and local levels, multiple executing entities, access limitations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Experienced staff in FPCU/FPIU, emergency procurement procedures, direct payments to third parties, regular audits

identified_risks.risk_description: Implementation in conflict areas, land acquisition risks, high mobility of displaced people, SEA/SH risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive E&S instruments, dedicated GRM for SEA/SH, specialist support at all levels

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited experience of implementing agencies with large-scale development interventions, weak technical capacities
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Mobile Support Teams, extensive capacity building, third-party implementation in HROC areas

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
ESS5: Land Acquisition and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM and separate GRM for SEA/SH complaints with confidential reporting protocols
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Strong focus on gender equality, women's participation in decision-making, addressing GBV, gender-inclusive consultations and planning processes

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Differentiated implementation approach based on conflict risk levels, periodic conflict assessments, community-driven planning to address tensions between IDPs and host communities, conflict-sensitive targeting mechanisms
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia faces multiple overlapping conflicts including regional conflicts, conflicts between armed groups and Federal Government, and local inter-communal violence over resources exacerbated by climate change
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security Risk Assessment and Management Plan, Mobile Support Teams, third-party implementation in high-risk areas, incident response protocols, quarterly security risk updates
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - project specifically targets 4.23 million IDPs (one of world's largest IDP populations), with 85% displaced by conflict and 15% by climate-related disasters. Focus on durable solutions including return, local integration, and relocation based on informed voluntary choice
